title: House of Many Ways - Chapter Five (Fragment 1)
text:
House of Many Ways

Chapter Five

I N WHICH C HARMAIN RECEIVES HER ANXIOUS PARENT

When Charmain woke, she discovered that Waif had planted her vast head on the bed, across Charmain’s legs. The rest of Waif was piled on the floor in a hairy white heap that filled most of the rest of the room.

“So you can’t go smaller on your own,” Charmain said. “I’ll have to think of something.”

Waif ’s answer was a series of giant wheezings, after which she appeared to go to sleep again. Charmain, with difficulty, dragged her legs out from under Waif ’s head and edged round Waif ’s vast body finding clean clothes and getting into them. When she came to do her hair, Charmain discovered that all the hairpins she usually put it up with seemed to have vanished, probably during her dive off the cliff. All she had left was a ribbon. Mother always insisted that respectable girls needed to have their hair in a neat knot on the top of their heads. Charmain had never worn her hair any other way.

“Oh, well,” she said to her reflection in the neat little mirror, “Mother’s not here, is she?” And she did her hair in a fat plait over one shoulder and fastened it with the ribbon. Like that, she thought her reflection looked nicer than usual, fuller in the face and less thin and grumpy. She nodded at her reflection and picked her way around Waif to get to the bathroom.

To her relief, the bathroom had thawed overnight. The room was full of soft dripping sounds from water dewing all the pipes, but nothing else seemed to be wrong until Charmain tried the taps. All four of them ran ice-cold water, no matter how long they ran for.

“I didn’t want a bath anyway,” Charmain said, as she went out into the corridor.

There was no sound from Peter. Charmain remembered Mother telling her that boys always were hard to wake in the morning. She did not let this worry her. She opened the door and turned left into the kitchen, into solid foam. Clots of foam and large single bubbles sailed past her into the corridor.

“Damnation!” Charmain said. She put her head down and her arms across her head and plowed into the room. It was as hot in there as her father’s bake house when he was baking for a big order. “Phew!” she said. “I suppose it takes days to use up a cake of soap.” After that she said nothing else, because her mouth filled with soapy froth when she opened it. Bubbles worked up her nose until she sneezed, causing a small foamy whirlwind. She collided with the table and heard another teapot fall down, but she plowed on until she ran into the laundry bags and heard the saucepans rattle on top of them. Then she knew where she was. She spared one hand from her face in order to fumble for the sink and then along the sink until she felt the back door under her fingers. She groped for the latch—for a moment she thought that had vanished in the night, until she realized it was on the other edge of the door—and finally flung the door open. Then she stood gulping in deep, soapy breaths and blinking her running, smarting, soap-filled eyes into a beautiful mild morning.

Bubbles sailed out past her in crowds. As her eyes cleared, Charmain stood admiring the way big shiny bubbles caught the sunlight as they soared against the green slopes of the mountains. Most of them, she noticed, seemed to pop when they got to the end of the yard, as if there was an invisible barrier there, but some sailed on and up and up as if they would go on forever. Charmain followed them up with her eyes, past brown cliffs and green slopes. One of those green slopes must be that meadow where she had met the lubbock, but she was unable to tell which. She let her eyes go on to the pale blue sky above the peaks. It was a truly lovely day.

vocabulary.definition: Experiencing worry or unease about something with an uncertain outcome.
vocabulary.example:

I N WHICH C HARMAIN RECEIVES HER ANXIOUS PARENT

When Charmain woke, she discovered that Waif had planted her vast head on the bed, across Charmain’s legs.

vocabulary.definition: The process of cooking food by dry heat in an oven.
vocabulary.example: It was as hot in there as her father’s bake house when he was baking for a big order.

vocabulary.definition: Steep rock faces, especially at the edge of the sea or a mountain.
vocabulary.example: When she came to do her hair, Charmain discovered that all the hairpins she usually put it up with seemed to have vanished, probably during her dive off the cliff.

vocabulary.definition: To come into violent contact with.
vocabulary.example: She collided with the table and heard another teapot fall down, but she plowed on until she ran into the laundry bags and heard the saucepans rattle on top of them.

vocabulary.definition: Forming or being covered with small drops of moisture (dew).
vocabulary.example: The room was full of soft dripping sounds from water dewing all the pipes, but nothing else seemed to be wrong until Charmain tried the taps.

vocabulary.definition: A mass of small bubbles formed in or on a liquid.
vocabulary.example: ” After that she said nothing else, because her mouth filled with soapy froth when she opened it.

vocabulary.definition: To try to hold or find something with the hands in an awkward way.
vocabulary.example: She spared one hand from her face in order to fumble for the sink and then along the sink until she felt the back door under her fingers.

vocabulary.definition: A collection of things thrown together in a pile.
vocabulary.example: The rest of Waif was piled on the floor in a hairy white heap that filled most of the rest of the room.

vocabulary.definition: An unseen boundary or obstacle that prevents passage or progress.
vocabulary.example: Most of them, she noticed, seemed to pop when they got to the end of the yard, as if there was an invisible barrier there, but some sailed on and up and up as if they would go on forever.

vocabulary.definition: A loop of rope, string, or hair fastened tightly.
vocabulary.example: Mother always insisted that respectable girls needed to have their hair in a neat knot on the top of their heads.

vocabulary.definition: Bags used to collect or transport clothes to be washed.
vocabulary.example: She collided with the table and heard another teapot fall down, but she plowed on until she ran into the laundry bags and heard the saucepans rattle on top of them.

vocabulary.definition: A field of grassland, especially one used for hay.
vocabulary.example: One of those green slopes must be that meadow where she had met the lubbock, but she was unable to tell which.

vocabulary.definition: Gentle in degree or effect; not harsh or severe.
vocabulary.example: Then she stood gulping in deep, soapy breaths and blinking her running, smarting, soap-filled eyes into a beautiful mild morning.

vocabulary.definition: To form words silently or without voice using the mouth.
vocabulary.example: ” After that she said nothing else, because her mouth filled with soapy froth when she opened it.

vocabulary.definition: A length of hair made up of three or more interlaced strands.
vocabulary.example: ” And she did her hair in a fat plait over one shoulder and fastened it with the ribbon.

vocabulary.definition: To make a rapid succession of short, sharp knocking sounds.
vocabulary.example: She collided with the table and heard another teapot fall down, but she plowed on until she ran into the laundry bags and heard the saucepans rattle on top of them.

vocabulary.definition: An image seen in a mirror or shiny surface.
vocabulary.example: “Oh, well,” she said to her reflection in the neat little mirror, “Mother’s not here, is she?

vocabulary.definition: Regarded by society as good, proper, or correct.
vocabulary.example: Mother always insisted that respectable girls needed to have their hair in a neat knot on the top of their heads.

vocabulary.definition: A long narrow strip of fabric, used for tying or decorating.
vocabulary.example: All she had left was a ribbon.

vocabulary.definition: Parts of a surface that lean or incline downward.
vocabulary.example: As her eyes cleared, Charmain stood admiring the way big shiny bubbles caught the sunlight as they soared against the green slopes of the mountains.

vocabulary.definition: Foamy bubbles produced by soap mixed with water.
vocabulary.example: ” After that she said nothing else, because her mouth filled with soapy froth when she opened it.

vocabulary.definition: To fly or rise high in the air.
vocabulary.example: As her eyes cleared, Charmain stood admiring the way big shiny bubbles caught the sunlight as they soared against the green slopes of the mountains.

vocabulary.definition: To melt or become liquid after being frozen.
vocabulary.example: To her relief, the bathroom had thawed overnight.

vocabulary.definition: To disappear suddenly and completely.
vocabulary.example: When she came to do her hair, Charmain discovered that all the hairpins she usually put it up with seemed to have vanished, probably during her dive off the cliff.

vocabulary.definition: A high-pitched whistling sound made during breathing, usually due to obstruction in the airways.
vocabulary.example:
”

Waif ’s answer was a series of giant wheezings, after which she appeared to go to sleep again.

vocabulary.definition: A rapidly rotating column of air, or a tumultuous or turbulent situation.
vocabulary.example: Bubbles worked up her nose until she sneezed, causing a small foamy whirlwind.
